noJoinedData.title: No joined data yet
noJoinedData.body: Scheduling efficiency requires both staff hours (Personalkollen) and revenue (POS) data for the same dates. Try expanding the date range.

status.leanHint: High revenue per hour — you're doing a lot with the hours scheduled.
status.on_targetHint: Revenue per labour hour within ±20% of your weekly average.
status.overstaffedHint: Revenue per hour is low — more hours scheduled than demand needs. Consider trimming.
status.no_dataHint: Fewer than 2 days of joined data for this weekday.

aiMap.emptyTitle: No PK shifts found for this window
aiMap.emptyBody: Either the schedule isn't published yet in Personalkollen, or the integration needs reconnecting.

hero.currentAfter: Current schedule · After applying {count, plural, one {# ready day} other {# ready days}}
hero.noForecast: No revenue forecast available — labour ratio can't be computed yet.
hero.daysReadyAmber: {ready, plural, one {# ready} other {# ready}} · {amber, plural, one {# amber} other {# amber}}

ready.title: Open Personalkollen and trim {count, plural, one {# day} other {# days}}
ready.openBtnTitle: Opens Personalkollen in a new tab — make the cuts there, then come back and click Apply to record them

legend.ready: {count, plural, one {# day · ready to apply} other {# days · ready to apply}}
legend.needsCall: {count, plural, one {# day · needs your call} other {# days · needs your call}}

row.amberDefault: Pattern says lighter, but you may know more — your call.
row.alignedBody: Schedule already aligned with the pattern.

actions.apply: Apply {count, plural, one {# ready day} other {# ready days}}

aiMap.drillStub: Decision drilldown for {date} — booking-vs-pattern view coming next.

headline.label: Labour ratio impact
headline.statesLine: Current schedule · {count, plural, one {after applying # ready day} other {after applying # ready days}}

ready.label: Ready to implement
ready.headline: Open Personalkollen and trim {count, plural, one {# day} other {# days}}

banner.strong: Daily % varies — week target is what matters.
banner.body: A rainy Monday will run hot. A sunny Friday will run cool. The week's average is what we optimise against. Daily % is shown for context, not as a goal.

legend.ready: {count, plural, one {# day · ready to apply} other {# days · ready to apply}}
legend.amber: {count, plural, one {# day · needs your call} other {# days · needs your call}}

weekGrid.emptyTitle: No PK shifts found for this window
weekGrid.emptyBody: Either the schedule isn't published yet in Personalkollen, or the integration needs reconnecting.
weekGrid.headSubtitle: Click a green cell to see why · Apply individually or all at once
weekGrid.headSubtitleMulti: {count} weeks · click any green cell to see why

panel.headline: Trim {hrs} from {day} — moves the week's labour % from {pctCur} to {pctIfDay}.

rationale.title: How the AI thinks about this week
rationale.p1Strong: Cuts only — we never recommend adding hours.
rationale.p1Body: The model can flag days where forecasted demand exceeds your coverage, but it won't suggest you add staff. That decision stays with you.
rationale.p2Strong: The week's average is the target — not each day.
rationale.p2Body: A rainy Monday will always run a higher labour % than a sunny Friday because revenue varies more than fixed minimum staffing. We optimise the week's average labour % against your {target}% target, treating each day's number as informational.

drill.subEyebrow: {count, plural, one {# day} other {# days}} · {staff} staff · {shifts} shifts · {hours}
drill.noStaffShifts: No staff shifts recorded for these dates.
drill.noDataForDay: No data for {day} in this period.
